identity.canonical_title: Radial Pressure Gradient and Acceleration Field

theory.canonical_statement: A centreward pressure decrease generates radial acceleration through the sourced pressure-gradient law; the Bernoulli and cross-geometry expressions record the surrounding model progression.
theory.technical_interpretation: From Bernoulli's equation \[ P + \frac{1}{2}\rho v^2 = \text{constant}, \] an increase of the fluid speed near the centre implies a pressure decrease toward the central opening, \[ \frac{\partial P}{\partial r} < 0. \] The resulting radial acceleration is \[ a_r = -\frac{1}{\rho}\frac{\partial P}{\partial r}. \] In the cross geometry of ID 0122, the four symmetric inflow channels reinforce one another so that the effective inward pull is amplified by a geometry-dependent factor, approximately $\sqrt{2}$ relative to a simple circular opening.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This ties the centreward velocity increase to a compensating pressure decrease.
This converts the pressure gradient into a direct force field on the flow.
This closes the progression by encoding the extra inward pull generated by the symmetric cross geometry.

publication_overrides.proof_latex:
\textbf{Statement:} The radial acceleration is determined by the sourced pressure-gradient relation
\[
a_r=-\frac{1}{\rho}\frac{\partial P}{\partial r}.
\]
\textbf{Logic Validation:}\\
\textbf{Proposition:} If \(\rho>0\) and \(\partial P/\partial r<0\), then \(a_r>0\) in the inward radial convention.\\
\textbf{Proof:} Dividing the negative pressure gradient by positive density and applying the leading minus sign yields an inward acceleration.\\
\textbf{VERDICT:} \ensuremath{\checkmark}\ \textbf{TRUE} --- source-backed radial pressure-gradient relation.\\
\textbf{Formal Status:} \ensuremath{\checkmark}\ THEORETICAL -- equation-grounded CHL proof obligation.\\
\textbf{Physical Status:} THEORETICAL -- application remains geometry- and measurement-dependent.

dictionary_sections.definition: A radial pressure-gradient acceleration field is the local motion produced when fluid pressure changes with radius. The sourced gradient supplies the directional cause of radial acceleration, scaled by fluid density.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: This entry describes a medium whose pressure is lower toward the centre of the modeled region. The fluid accelerates toward decreasing pressure, connecting a measurable profile, \(P(r)\), to a dynamical response, \(a_r\), before the later geometry comparison is applied.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: For positive density, acceleration has the opposite sign from the pressure derivative. Thus \(\partial P/\partial r<0\) gives centreward acceleration, and increasing density with the gradient fixed reduces its magnitude.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: The approved aligned source identifies this radial pressure-gradient law and defines \(a_r\), \(P\), and \(\rho\). Bernoulli and cross-geometry relations remain supporting progression material.
dictionary_sections.technical_interpretation: The factor \(-1/\rho\) converts pressure gradient into acceleration per unit mass. This is a local response law before global boundary or comparative geometry conditions are added.
dictionary_sections.equation_grounding: \(a_r\) is radial acceleration produced by the pressure gradient; \(P\) is fluid pressure; and \(\rho\) is fluid density. These meanings are source-approved for the canonical law shown above.
dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: This law moves the arc from scalar pressure distribution to directed motion, giving the derivation and proof layers a concrete physical relation.

tzp_thesaurus.meaning: The rate at which pressure changes along the radial coordinate.
Radial motion directed toward decreasing radius when pressure falls inward.
The inertial scaling quantity moderating acceleration for a fixed gradient.

publication_overrides.equation_support_summary: For positive density, a centreward pressure decrease gives centreward radial acceleration; the remaining progression terms compare that anchored response across the modeled geometry.

approved_source_enrichment.method: formula-normalized raw export alignment followed by manual review

approved_source_enrichment.limitation: The aligned passage validates the radial acceleration relation and symbol roles; it does not independently validate the cross-geometry amplification factor.
